text.en: United Kingdom
text.th: United Kingdom

text.en: The top 15% of AI users report stronger performance reviews, pay increases and substantial time savings. The challenge now is upskilling the remaining 85% to enable everyone to use AI to unlock personal progression.
text.th: The top 15% of AI users report stronger performance reviews, pay increases and substantial time savings. The challenge now is upskilling the remaining 85% to enable everyone to use AI to unlock personal progression.

body.alt: Four illustrated characters representing different professional roles, including a scientist, technician, explorer, and observer.

text.en: The UK’s AI adoption: fast, but uneven
text.th: The UK’s AI adoption: fast, but uneven

text.en: We’ve worked with Public First on one of the most comprehensive UK AI adoption studies to date. The research reveals that workplace AI adoption has doubled in the past year, (up to 73%, from 34% in 2025). Yet, this momentum reveals an uneven adoption curve, where only a minority - the top 15% - are significantly more likely to report promotions, pay rises and faster career progression.
text.th: We’ve worked with Public First on one of the most comprehensive UK AI adoption studies to date. The research reveals that workplace AI adoption has doubled in the past year, (up to 73%, from 34% in 2025). Yet, this momentum reveals an uneven adoption curve, where only a minority - the top 15% - are significantly more likely to report promotions, pay rises and faster career progression.

body.alt: Infographic showing 73% of the British workforce now use AI, which has more than doubled from 34% in 2025 to 73% in 2026.

text.en: Our analysis reveals that AI usage is a spectrum, with most of the UK’s workforce still stuck in early-stage adoption. We’ve segmented the workforce into four progressive stages:
text.th: Our analysis reveals that AI usage is a spectrum, with most of the UK’s workforce still stuck in early-stage adoption. We’ve segmented the workforce into four progressive stages:

text.en: ‘AI Spectators’ (10%) - People who aren’t yet experimenting with AI
text.th: ‘AI Spectators’ (10%) - People who aren’t yet experimenting with AI

text.en: ‘AI Experimenters’ (38%) - Beginners testing the waters with simple tasks
text.th: ‘AI Experimenters’ (38%) - Beginners testing the waters with simple tasks

text.en: ‘The AI Practitioners’ (37%) - Intermediates using AI as a reliable daily tool.
text.th: ‘The AI Practitioners’ (37%) - Intermediates using AI as a reliable daily tool.

text.en: ‘AI Trailblazers’ (15%) - Advanced users pushing boundaries and finding entirely new ways to work.
text.th: ‘AI Trailblazers’ (15%) - Advanced users pushing boundaries and finding entirely new ways to work.

body.alt: Infographic card showing that 10% are AI Spectators who do not use AI in their daily lives.
Infographic card showing that 38% are AI Experimenters who use AI occasionally for quick questions and simple drafting tasks.
Infographic card showing that 37% are AI Practitioners who use AI regularly for drafting, research, and problem-solving.
Infographic card showing that 15% are AI Trailblazers who use AI in deeper, creative ways like automating tasks and using agents.

text.en: The AI Trailblazer advantage
text.th: The AI Trailblazer advantage

text.en: The UK’s AI Trailblazers are creating a new benchmark for modern work and saving almost 8 hours across both their personal and professional lives - effectively gaining an extra day each working week.
text.th: The UK’s AI Trailblazers are creating a new benchmark for modern work and saving almost 8 hours across both their personal and professional lives - effectively gaining an extra day each working week.

text.en: The research found that even after accounting for differences in age, sector, gender, ethnicity, education and business size, deeper AI use is associated with greater professional momentum. Trailblazers are:
text.th: The research found that even after accounting for differences in age, sector, gender, ethnicity, education and business size, deeper AI use is associated with greater professional momentum. Trailblazers are:

text.en: 84% more likely to have been promoted in the past year.
text.th: 84% more likely to have been promoted in the past year.

text.en: 88% more likely to achieve a positive performance review.
text.th: 88% more likely to achieve a positive performance review.

text.en: 55% more likely to secure a pay rise.
text.th: 55% more likely to secure a pay rise.

text.en: But, this deeper AI use is unevenly spread across age groups, genders and geographical location. The longer we wait to take action the more that this gap will grow.
text.th: But, this deeper AI use is unevenly spread across age groups, genders and geographical location. The longer we wait to take action the more that this gap will grow.

text.en: The good news is that these disparities are entirely addressable. Reaching this advanced level doesn't require deep technical knowledge or coding expertise. Anyone can become a Trailblazer.
text.th: The good news is that these disparities are entirely addressable. Reaching this advanced level doesn't require deep technical knowledge or coding expertise. Anyone can become a Trailblazer.

body.alt: A chart titled "Even after controls, AI Trailblazers report better work outcomes" listing job performance categories.

text.en: Breaking down the barriers
text.th: Breaking down the barriers

text.en: The real challenge is converting everyday experimentation into a level of AI literacy that unlocks career progression and, in turn, nationwide economic growth.
text.th: The real challenge is converting everyday experimentation into a level of AI literacy that unlocks career progression and, in turn, nationwide economic growth.

text.en: So, how do we help the other 85% unlock these benefits? The barriers holding people back are surprisingly easy to overcome, with collective focus. They are, for the most part, either behavioral, cognitive or organisational:
text.th: So, how do we help the other 85% unlock these benefits? The barriers holding people back are surprisingly easy to overcome, with collective focus. They are, for the most part, either behavioral, cognitive or organisational:

text.en: Behavioral - The "One-and-Done" habit: Most casual users haven’t gotten into the habit of using AI effectively. Many aren’t yet iterating prompts, matching the right tool to the task, or are unsure of how to get started with multi-modal capabilities (text, visual, and audio inputs and outputs) and agentic workflows (where AI autonomously plans and executes multi-step tasks).
text.th: Behavioral - The "One-and-Done" habit: Most casual users haven’t gotten into the habit of using AI effectively. Many aren’t yet iterating prompts, matching the right tool to the task, or are unsure of how to get started with multi-modal capabilities (text, visual, and audio inputs and outputs) and agentic workflows (where AI autonomously plans and executes multi-step tasks).

text.en: Cognitive - The traditional "Search Box" mindset: Millions of users naturally apply their familiar search habits to AI tools, instead of treating it as a creative partner. Despite AI's highly collaborative nature, only 37% of previous users have ever asked an AI to help them write a better prompt to quickly achieve more effective results.
text.th: Cognitive - The traditional "Search Box" mindset: Millions of users naturally apply their familiar search habits to AI tools, instead of treating it as a creative partner. Despite AI's highly collaborative nature, only 37% of previous users have ever asked an AI to help them write a better prompt to quickly achieve more effective results.

text.en: Organisational - The "Permission to Prompt" gap: Many workers are waiting for explicit permission to use AI. Only one-third of AI users have clear professional guidance to help them use AI confidently, and fewer than half know who to ask about responsible use.
text.th: Organisational - The "Permission to Prompt" gap: Many workers are waiting for explicit permission to use AI. Only one-third of AI users have clear professional guidance to help them use AI confidently, and fewer than half know who to ask about responsible use.

text.en: Building a nation of AI Trailblazers
text.th: Building a nation of AI Trailblazers

text.en: Levelling up starts with knowing where you stand. That’s why today, Public First is launching the AI skills quiz, an interactive diagnostic tool that lets you benchmark your skills against the rest of the population, learn more about the type of AI user you are and get actionable skills to instantly elevate your AI use and help unlock career growth.
text.th: Levelling up starts with knowing where you stand. That’s why today, Public First is launching the AI skills quiz, an interactive diagnostic tool that lets you benchmark your skills against the rest of the population, learn more about the type of AI user you are and get actionable skills to instantly elevate your AI use and help unlock career growth.

body.alt: AI Skills Quiz landing page titled "What kind of AI user are you?" featuring four categories and a start button.

text.en: Our nationwide AI upskilling initiative, AI Works for Britain, aims to tackle this specific uneven adoption challenge, and builds on our Google Digital Garage programme - which has already trained over 1.2 million people over the past decade. This is a key element of our partnership with the Government to help achieve their goal of training 10 million workers in AI skills by 2030. Together, we can close the adoption gap, build a nation of AI Trailblazers, and help every worker unlock their full potential.
text.th: Our nationwide AI upskilling initiative, AI Works for Britain, aims to tackle this specific uneven adoption challenge, and builds on our Google Digital Garage programme - which has already trained over 1.2 million people over the past decade. This is a key element of our partnership with the Government to help achieve their goal of training 10 million workers in AI skills by 2030. Together, we can close the adoption gap, build a nation of AI Trailblazers, and help every worker unlock their full potential.

text.en: The impact of Google’s products and services
text.th: The impact of Google’s products and services

text.en: One of the most important ways we can enable deeper AI adoption and unlock growth and progression is through helpful AI tools. In 2025, Google’s tools supported £140 billion in economic activity across the UK - equivalent to the economy of Greater Manchester. Over 40% of that activity (£60bn) comes from empowering British SMBs who are using our tools to innovate and grow. Our products such as Search, Android, Cloud and YouTube are already saving British workers 51 million hours a week - roughly the weekly output of the National Health Service’s (NHS) entire workforce.
text.th: One of the most important ways we can enable deeper AI adoption and unlock growth and progression is through helpful AI tools. In 2025, Google’s tools supported £140 billion in economic activity across the UK - equivalent to the economy of Greater Manchester. Over 40% of that activity (£60bn) comes from empowering British SMBs who are using our tools to innovate and grow. Our products such as Search, Android, Cloud and YouTube are already saving British workers 51 million hours a week - roughly the weekly output of the National Health Service’s (NHS) entire workforce.

Three infographic cards tracking metrics for British workforce AI adoption, user benefits, and Google digital training.
